revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hongō Haniwa Kiln Site]][[ja:本郷埴輪窯跡]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Hongō Haniwa Kiln ruins | native_name =本郷埴輪窯跡 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hongō Haniwa Kiln ruins | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hongō Haniwa Kiln ruins | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|13|46|N|139|04|47|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|qid=Q596486|lt=Fujioka, Gunma|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = ancient kiln | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Hongō Haniwa Kiln ruins'''|本郷埴輪窯跡|Hongō haniwa kama ato}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} with the ruins of an ancient [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Kiln|en|Kiln|qid=Q1724341|lt=Kiln|1=Kiln}} located in what is now the Hongō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|qid=Q596486|lt=Fujioka|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162311|title=本郷埴輪窯跡|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] }}</ref>
==Overview== The Hongō Haniwa Kiln was located is located on the slope of a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} facing the Kanna River on the south side of modern Fujioka. This area was inhabited by the "Hajibe", a caste of potters who traditionally produced {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} earthenware and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', and there is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} nearby dedicated to the ancestors of the Hajibe clan. The existence of the kiln was confirmed in 1911, but an academic [[archaeological excavation]] was not made until 1943. At that time, more than a dozen kilns ruins were found at intervals of four meters along the terraced cliffs, of which two were excavated in detail. These were found to be a type of ''[[:en:noborigama|noborigama]]'' kiln, utilizing the natural slope of the hill, with a total length of ten meters. The kiln is divided into a trumpet-shaped six-meter ventilation path and a 2-meter combustion section, which was cylindrical and which had an inclination of about 30 degrees. The walls and floors were lined with clay. In front of the kiln area were the shards of many ''haniwa'', including horse-shaped versions. It is estimated that these kilns date from the 6th century to the early 7th century, and supplied ''haniwa'' for use in ''[[kofun]]'' Kofuns across a wide region of the Kantō area.
The site was backfilled after excavation and is covered in forest, It is about 30 minutes walk from {{ill|Gunma-Fujioka Station|en|Gunma-Fujioka Station|qid=Q845730|lt=Gunma-Fujioka Station|1=Gunma-Fujioka Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hachikō Line|en|Hachikō Line|qid=Q1372071|lt=Hachikō Line|1=Hachikō Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|qid=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/hongouhaniwagama.html Fujioka City official guide] {{in lang|ja}}
